# UC-17.4.1 · Cloudflare WAF Managed Rule Block Rate by Rule ID (`cloudflare:json`)

## Description

Managed rules evolve weekly; tracking blocks per rule ID shows tuning regressions and false-positive spikes after rule set upgrades.

## Value

Security engineering prioritizes which exceptions to file with Cloudflare support and proves value of managed rule packs.

## Implementation

Configure Logpush jobs per dataset; route all into a unified `cloudflare:json` sourcetype or use transforms to normalize. Use `spath` for PascalCase fields. Create macros per zone name.

## SPL

```spl
index=waf sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| spath RuleID
| spath Action
| where Action="block" OR match(Action,"(?i)deny")
| stats count by RuleID, ClientRequestHost
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (rule ID), timechart (block rate), table (zone, path prefix).

## References

- [Cloudflare Logpush documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpush/)
- [Splunkbase — Cloudflare App for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4501)
